title: Brush With Greatness

jokes.text: The food, the grog.... They threw a brunch with fresh fruit and the most delicious melon.
jokes.punchline: Calling watermelon 'the most delicious melon' as if it's profound
jokes.explanation: Krusty's vapid endorsement language - treating obvious watermelon description as meaningful praise

jokes.text: I told them you would. Don't make me a liar!
jokes.setup: Krusty asking kids to pack the venue
jokes.punchline: Desperate plea revealing he's already promised them attendance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty characterization - sleazy showbiz desperation masked as kid-friendly appeal

jokes.text: This is a rather shameless promotion. -Worked on me.
jokes.setup: Lisa critiquing the obvious commercial manipulation
jokes.punchline: Bart admitting he fell for it anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa/Bart dynamic - her awareness vs his susceptibility to marketing

jokes.text: Will you take us to Mt. Splashmore? -No. Will you take us to Mt. Splashmore? -No!
jokes.punchline: Homer's increasingly emphatic refusal
jokes.explanation: Standard family comedy setup - predictable but functional escalation

jokes.text: If I take you, will you two shut up? -Yeah! -Of course! -Will you take us to Mt. Splashmore? -Yes!
jokes.setup: Homer's conditional offer and kids' agreement
jokes.punchline: Kids immediately continuing the behavior they promised to stop
jokes.explanation: Perfect kid logic - immediately breaking their promise while technically getting what they want

jokes.text: Homer, that suit doesn't leave much to the imagination.
jokes.setup: Visual of Homer in tight swimsuit
jokes.punchline: Marge's polite way of saying he looks awful
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge diplomacy - euphemistic criticism of Homer's appearance

jokes.text: Kids, I'm moving the seat back. -It is back.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming he'll adjust seat for comfort
jokes.punchline: Revelation that it's already fully back
jokes.explanation: Efficient fat joke - Homer's size revealed through car logistics

jokes.text: Everybody, stick together. We don't want to get separ-- D'oh!
jokes.punchline: Immediately getting separated/injured himself
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer irony - authority figure immediately failing at his own advice

jokes.text: H-2-Whoa! -Whoa!
jokes.punchline: H2O/Whoa pun
jokes.explanation: Obvious pun - functional but predictable wordplay

jokes.text: Ay caramba! Okay. Turn on the waterworks, babe. Mommy! I want my mommy!
jokes.setup: Bart's bravado at the top of scary slide
jokes.punchline: Immediately switching to fake crying for sympathy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart characterization - brave facade instantly dropped for strategic manipulation

jokes.text: Step aside. Spread out. Lost kid coming through. Pardon me. Moving to the front. Nice work, babe.
jokes.punchline: His confident navigation and congratulating himself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bart scam sequence - the self-congratulation sells his calculating nature perfectly

jokes.text: The hell with this! Slide inspector coming through! Move to the right. Move! I'm here for your safety. -God bless that man!
jokes.punchline: Making up authority position and being believed
jokes.explanation: Homer's lazy authority scam working perfectly - people's gullibility pays off his impatience

jokes.text: What? Come on, you stupid tube!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to use water slide equipment
jokes.punchline: Blaming the inanimate object for his incompetence
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer frustration, predictable blame-shifting

jokes.text: There's a jam in Delta sector. Too big to be human. Send kids to dislodge it.
jokes.punchline: Casually sending children to fix human blockage
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark corporate efficiency - treating Homer like equipment malfunction

jokes.text: Can't breathe. Too many children.
jokes.punchline: Homer's claustrophobic complaint
jokes.explanation: Absurd situation payoff - Homer's predicament described matter-of-factly

jokes.text: On the lighter side of news, and I use the term loosely....
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'lighter' referencing his obesity
jokes.explanation: Competent wordplay - news anchor acknowledging his own pun adds self-awareness

jokes.text: Isn't that what you said before the recall of Krusty-Brand Mayonnaise?
jokes.punchline: Reference to previous Krusty product failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty brand consistency - his corporate negligence as running gag

jokes.text: That question is out of bounds. Interview's over!
jokes.setup: Tough question about his product safety record
jokes.punchline: Abrupt interview termination
jokes.explanation: Krusty's defensive corporate behavior - deflection through authority

jokes.text: Well, am I? Forgive us, but it takes time to properly sugarcoat a response.
jokes.setup: Homer asking family if he's overweight
jokes.punchline: Lisa's diplomatic explanation for the silence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa intelligence - explaining the family's awkward silence with precision

jokes.text: Fifty-five? My God! Three hundred and-- A hundred and fifty? Oh, my God! Two hundred and sixty pounds! I'm a big, fat pig!
jokes.punchline: His shocked reactions to different weight readings
jokes.explanation: Homer's math confusion creates escalating shock - his inability to read the scale properly

jokes.text: Marge, no one gains 30 pounds of bone!
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting he has big bones
jokes.punchline: Homer's logical rebuttal
jokes.explanation: Homer's rare moment of logical clarity - debunking Marge's polite excuse

jokes.text: ...there'll be no pork chop too succulent, no donut too tasty...no pizza too laden with delicious toppings...to prevent me from reaching my ideal weight!
jokes.punchline: Describing tempting food while claiming to resist it
jokes.explanation: Homer's self-defeating diet speech - getting distracted by food descriptions mid-commitment

jokes.text: As God is my witness, I'll always be hungry again!
jokes.punchline: Misquoting Gone with the Wind to be about perpetual hunger
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cultural reference subversion - Homer turning Scarlett's determination into acceptance of permanent hunger

jokes.text: D'oh! Shut up!
jokes.setup: Homer's stomach rumbling during his diet speech
jokes.punchline: Yelling at his own stomach
jokes.explanation: Homer's body betraying his resolve immediately - arguing with his own stomach

jokes.text: This old attic's kind of spooky, isn't it? I said, isn't it? Don't ever do that again! Okay.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to get Lisa's attention in spooky attic
jokes.punchline: Lisa apparently scaring him, then his immediate submission
jokes.explanation: Sibling dynamic - Bart's bravado immediately deflated by Lisa's response

jokes.text: Homer, I found your weights. -Oh, the Glutimus Maximizer.
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous brand name combining 'glutes' with 'maximus'
jokes.explanation: Good fake product name that sounds like real exercise equipment marketing

jokes.text: Who's the mop-top with the schnoz? -That's Ringo Starr.
jokes.punchline: His crude description of the famous Beatle
jokes.explanation: Homer's philistine perspective - reducing Beatles icon to crude physical description

jokes.text: I guess she thought he was cute. What? Hey!
jokes.setup: Homer casually dismissing Marge's teenage crush
jokes.punchline: His delayed jealous realization
jokes.explanation: Homer's slow processing - delayed jealous reaction to decades-old crush

jokes.text: Wait, I've been setting my drinks on these things!
jokes.setup: Marge explaining rice cakes are food
jokes.punchline: Homer's realization he's been using them as coasters
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer food ignorance - rice cakes so flavorless he didn't recognize them as edible

jokes.text: Hello? Hello! Hello, taste? Where are you?
jokes.punchline: Calling out for taste like a missing person
jokes.explanation: Homer's personification of taste as something that abandoned him - creative food complaint

jokes.text: Another one of that bongo beater! -Mr. Schindler--
jokes.setup: Art teacher seeing Ringo Starr painting
jokes.punchline: Dismissive reference to world's most famous drummer
jokes.explanation: Art teacher's pretentious dismissal - reducing Beatles drummer to 'bongo beater'

jokes.text: One might've used this canvas to paint a masterpiece. Instead, you've soiled it forever.
jokes.punchline: Overdramatic condemnation of amateur art
jokes.explanation: Pretentious art teacher cruelty - treating canvas like desecrated sacred ground

jokes.text: What do you think, Homer? -Do I have to do anything? -No. -Great! Fine! Go nuts!
jokes.setup: Marge asking Homer about art classes
jokes.punchline: His only concern being whether effort is required
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer laziness - support contingent solely on his non-participation

jokes.text: I yearn to tell the story of a young Hindu...pushed too far by Quik-E-Mart bandits: It's Hands off My Jerky, Turkey.
jokes.punchline: Revenge fantasy with terrible pun title
jokes.explanation: Apu's immigrant experience filtered through action movie tropes - personal pain as B-movie

jokes.text: Clever title. -Thanks. My brother thought of it.
jokes.punchline: Apu deflecting credit to his brother
jokes.explanation: Marge's Minnesota nice meeting Apu's family collaboration on terrible puns

jokes.text: What? The man was a fool! -I admire the force of his conviction.
jokes.setup: New teacher calling old teacher foolish
jokes.punchline: Marge's diplomatic praise of the critic who destroyed her confidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge diplomacy - finding positive spin on someone who crushed her dreams

jokes.text: The Heavy Hands only work if you move. -I can't. Oh, really?
jokes.punchline: Implied physical forcing of movement
jokes.explanation: Standard exercise equipment torture - Homer's immediate defeat by fitness gear

jokes.text: Lincoln, I've been thinking What have you been drinking? Is it water? Is it wine? Oh, my gosh, it's turpentine
jokes.punchline: Improvised song about paint mixing confusion
jokes.explanation: Marge's artistic enthusiasm - silly impromptu song about painting supplies

jokes.text: Bravo! Walk away. Now it belongs to the ages. Not another stroke! Oh, one more. That's it! Perfect!
jokes.setup: Art teacher's dramatic pronouncement about stopping
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself multiple times
jokes.explanation: Pretentious art teacher's indecision - dramatic pronouncements undercut by immediate reversals

jokes.text: Marge, please. I don't take praise very well!
jokes.punchline: Claiming inability to handle compliments
jokes.explanation: Perfect character hypocrisy - narcissist claiming humility while fishing for compliments

jokes.text: Marge, please. I don't take praise very well!
jokes.punchline: Rejecting praise while obviously loving attention
jokes.explanation: Good character hypocrisy, obviously false modesty from an attention-seeker

jokes.text: I'm a work of art! Last Supper, eat your heart out.
jokes.setup: Homer being chosen as painting subject
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to da Vinci masterpiece
jokes.explanation: Homer's grandiose self-regard - positioning himself as rival to religious art masterpiece

jokes.text: What matchbook art school did you flunk out of, housepainter?
jokes.punchline: Elaborate insult about amateur art education
jokes.explanation: Burns' elaborate cruelty - specific reference to correspondence art schools shows his cultural snobbery

jokes.text: This is as half-baked as your idea about me having children.
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to the terrible idea of Burns reproducing
jokes.explanation: Burns' casual evil - treating his genetic legacy as obviously terrible idea

jokes.text: Excellent! Once again, the wheel has turned...and Dame Fortune has hugged Montgomery Burns to her sweet bosom.
jokes.punchline: Overly poetic celebration of minor convenience
jokes.explanation: Burns' flowery evil speech - treating simple problem solving as cosmic destiny

jokes.text: I told you to show support. -Way to go, Dad! -You look marvelous!
jokes.setup: Kids' lackluster response to Homer's weight loss
jokes.punchline: Forced enthusiasm after maternal coaching
jokes.explanation: Family dynamics - Marge orchestrating fake enthusiasm, kids complying mechanically

jokes.text: Homer Simpson went on a diet. -Oh, my God! And I just bought a boat!
jokes.punchline: Donut seller's financial panic about boat purchase
jokes.explanation: Perfect economic absurdity - one man's diet crashing entire donut-based economy

jokes.text: Ring Go? -He was the drummer for the Beatles. Beatles, eh? Oh, yes. I remember their off-key caterwauling on the old Sullivan show.
jokes.punchline: Dismissive description of the most famous band in history
jokes.explanation: Burns' cultural disconnect - mispronouncing Ringo and dismissing Beatles as noise

jokes.text: Can you make me beautiful? -That won't be a problem. -I'm no matinee idol. Maybe not, but I have the gift of seeing inner beauty.
jokes.setup: Burns asking to be made beautiful in art
jokes.punchline: Marge's diplomatic response about finding inner beauty in anyone
jokes.explanation: Marge's impossible task - promising to find beauty in the show's embodiment of evil

jokes.text: There's an original sentiment. / Take it outside. I'm male-modeling.
jokes.punchline: Burns' sarcastic response and Homer's macho posing terminology
jokes.explanation: Burns' wit versus Homer's obliviousness. 'Male-modeling' is perfect Homer self-aggrandizement

jokes.text: Something's on my leg! Get it off! -Mr. Burns, she's just a baby!
jokes.punchline: His terrified reaction to harmless infant contact
jokes.explanation: Burns' inhuman nature - treating baby like dangerous creature

jokes.text: So Ziggy goes to the repair shop. There's a sign reading 'Out of Order.' Oh, Ziggy, will you ever win?
jokes.punchline: His emotional investment in the perpetually unlucky character
jokes.explanation: Homer's sincere connection to lowbrow comic strip - finding pathos in simple cartoon

jokes.text: Did he have spots all over his body? -I heard that!
jokes.setup: Marge asking Lisa about Burns' appearance
jokes.punchline: Burns overhearing from another room
jokes.explanation: Standard eavesdropping joke - Burns' vanity making him hypersensitive to appearance discussions

jokes.text: Should I leave too, sir? Of course not, Smithers. You're like a doctor.
jokes.setup: Smithers asking if he should leave during Burns' changing
jokes.punchline: Burns' medical professional comparison
jokes.explanation: Burns/Smithers dynamic - rationalizing inappropriate intimacy with professional comparison

jokes.text: He's not just my boss. He's my best friend.
jokes.setup: Smithers defending his devotion to Burns
jokes.punchline: Pathetic one-sided friendship declaration
jokes.explanation: Smithers' tragic devotion - calling his abusive boss his best friend

jokes.text: PS: Forgive the late reply.
jokes.punchline: Apologizing for 25-year delay
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd understatement - treating quarter-century delay as minor courtesy issue

jokes.text: Even if it takes 20 more years, I'm going to answer every one of them.
jokes.punchline: Commitment to continue impossibly slow pace
jokes.explanation: Ringo's admirable dedication rendered absurd by mathematical impossibility

jokes.text: Marge! I'm two-thirty-nine and feeling fine I'm using the original notches!
jokes.punchline: Pride in fitting old belt notches
jokes.explanation: Homer's simple pleasure - belt notches as milestone achievement

jokes.text: You're the fattest thing I've ever seen...and I've been on safari.
jokes.punchline: Safari comparison for scale reference
jokes.explanation: Burns' elaborate cruelty - comparing Homer unfavorably to African megafauna

jokes.text: If you need me, I'll be in the refrigerator.
jokes.setup: Homer's emotional response to Burns' insult
jokes.punchline: Seeking comfort literally inside food source
jokes.explanation: Homer's food relationship - treating refrigerator as emotional refuge

jokes.text: Don't listen to him! He's a mean little SOB.
jokes.punchline: Rare moment of Homer correctly identifying Burns' character
jokes.explanation: Homer's rare clarity - accurately assessing his boss for once

jokes.text: Yes, we do have hamburgers and fries in England...but we call French fries 'chips.' Love, Ringo.
jokes.punchline: Patiently explaining basic cultural differences
jokes.explanation: Ringo's patient fan service - addressing presumably silly American questions

jokes.text: Ladies and gentlemen, may I invite you to behold... Montgomery Burns?
jokes.punchline: The dramatic presentation of what turns out to be an unflattering portrait
jokes.explanation: Sets up the major reveal moment, standard formal presentation

jokes.text: I guess I wanted to show that beneath Mr. Burns' fearsome head...with its cruel lips, spiteful tongue and evil brain...there was a frail, withered body, perhaps not long for this world...as vulnerable and beautiful as any of God's creatures.
jokes.punchline: Cataloguing Burns' evil features while claiming to show his beauty
jokes.explanation: Marge's diplomatic brutality - describing Burns' evil features while claiming charitable intent

jokes.text: He's bad, but he'll die. So I like it.
jokes.setup: Art critic analyzing Marge's Burns portrait
jokes.punchline: Appreciating art for its mortality reminder
jokes.explanation: Brilliant art criticism reduction - finding aesthetic value in villain's mortality

jokes.text: And incidentally, thanks for not making fun of my genitalia. I thought I did.
jokes.setup: Burns thanking Marge for artistic dignity
jokes.punchline: Marge's innocent admission of anatomical mockery
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode-ending subversion - Marge's innocent honesty revealing she did mock his anatomy

tmdb_overview: Marge rediscovers her interest in art and takes up painting. When Mr. Burns hires her to paint his official portrait, Marge tries but fails to find his "inner beauty."
insight: Marge's art storyline sustains 66 jokes across 22 minutes—a Season 2 density peak.
